content:
Stories (continued) — HRREC-82977

StoryGap score (%)PM lensQA lensXO lensVerdictSuggested missing BDD (Given/When/Then)

HRREC-91979
PH: Recruiter does not have access to the compose email task

38% — PM slice value clear; QA tenant limits; XO catalog present but per-hook proof still SUV work.

  • Jira — PH: Recruiter does not have access to the compose email task: confirm acceptance scenarios still match recruiter language after recent edits.
  • Salomon — Agency / UDMF: duplicate agency submissions depend on BP CRF vs UDMF-aware rule; isolation tests need explicit internal vs agency accounts.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-91979 (PH: Recruiter does not have access to the compose e…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • Functional knowledge — Consent and audit expectations for candidate-visible email still apply—tie any new surface to comms policy review.
  • Jira — Exercise negatives implied by PH: Recruiter does not have access to the compose email task: record tenant SKU (SSP growth) and mail lane used.
  • Salomon — Email Analytics may reject empty subject lines—pair blank-body template issues with vendor status rows when reproducing.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-91979 (PH: Recruiter does not have access to the compose e…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • XO MCP — row focus HRREC-91979: PH: Recruiter does not have access to the compose email task — map SUV bindings for this slice; Tier B catalog search only (no per-hook proof in this pass).
  • XO MCP — searched Recruiting Email PRU/PU paths (Candidate Email create, External/Internal candidate parms) for send/receive wiring.
  • XO MCP — risk: duplicate REST paths for similar compose actions—assert the exact endpoint used by this UI path.
  • Finding: HRREC-91979 — Cross-story overlap on compose/send paths—risk of conflicting mocks unless QE shares one baseline tenant recipe.
  • Recommended next step: Log Default SMTP vs override and Recruiting Communications template state in each defect.

Scenario: REST parity spot-check (key-specific)
Given tenant staged for HRREC-91979 with logged mail mode
When the recruiter performs the behaviour described in: Recruiter does not have access to the compose email task
Then UI, persisted thread state, and captured network payloads align for this slice

HRREC-91980
PH: Recruiter sees the panel expand when clicking Add to compose an email

38% — PM slice value clear; QA tenant limits; XO catalog present but per-hook proof still SUV work.

  • Jira — PH: Recruiter sees the panel expand when clicking Add to compose an email: confirm acceptance scenarios still match recruiter language after recent edits.
  • Salomon — Recruiting Communications / tenant templates: blank-body risk when subject or body missing or template overrides recruiter draft (internal precedent on grid sends).
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-91980 (PH: Recruiter sees the panel expand when clicking A…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • Functional knowledge — Consent and audit expectations for candidate-visible email still apply—tie any new surface to comms policy review.
  • Jira — Exercise negatives implied by PH: Recruiter sees the panel expand when clicking Add to compose an e…: record tenant SKU (SSP growth) and mail lane used.
  • Salomon — Email Analytics may reject empty subject lines—pair blank-body template issues with vendor status rows when reproducing.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-91980 (PH: Recruiter sees the panel expand when clicking A…): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • XO MCP — row focus HRREC-91980: PH: Recruiter sees the panel expand when clicking Add to compose an email — map SUV bindings for this slice; Tier B catalog search only (no per-hook proof in this pass).
  • XO MCP — searched cl: Conversation (and related conversational UI classes) as anchor for thread container work.
  • XO MCP — risk: attachment handling spans multiple services—confirm which layer enforces limits for this flow.
  • Finding: HRREC-91980 — Accessibility and empty states are easy to regress—pair visual checks with keyboard-only navigation notes.
  • Recommended next step: Run one cross-browser smoke on the primary path after each merge train batch for this epic.

Scenario: REST parity spot-check (key-specific)
Given tenant staged for HRREC-91980 with logged mail mode
When the recruiter performs the behaviour described in: Recruiter sees the panel expand when clicking Add to compose an email

HRREC-91987
PH: Recruiter can select To From Subject (UI) when composing an email

38% — PM slice value clear; QA tenant limits; XO catalog present but per-hook proof still SUV work.

  • Jira — PH: Recruiter can select To From Subject (UI) when composing an email: confirm acceptance scenarios still match recruiter language after recent edits.
  • Salomon — Purge: Recruiting Communications PDT removes conversation messages, participants, recruiting email attachments, notification events—align recruiter-visible UI after purge with privacy comms.
  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-91987 (PH: Recruiter can select To From Subject (UI) when …): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • Functional knowledge — Consent and audit expectations for candidate-visible email still apply—tie any new surface to comms policy review.
  • Jira — Exercise negatives implied by PH: Recruiter can select To From Subject (UI) when composing an email: record tenant SKU (SSP growth) and mail lane used.

  • Deployment Agent — For HRREC-91987 (PH: Recruiter can select To From Subject (UI) when …): log whether notifications use Default SMTP vs any override in Edit Tenant Setup - Notifications; capture Recruiting Communications template active state when reproducing mail defects.
  • XO MCP — row focus HRREC-91987: PH: Recruiter can select To From Subject (UI) when composing an email — map SUV bindings for this slice; Tier B catalog search only (no per-hook proof in this pass).
  • XO MCP — searched cl: Conversation Message (+ Derived, Status, Sender) for delivery tags, read/unread, purge visibility.
  • XO MCP — risk: validate which XO class owns the recruiter-visible field you are changing—avoid fixing the wrong layer.
  • Finding: HRREC-91987 — Slice looks UI-led in Jira; backend touchpoints (notifications, persistence) need one SUV confirmation pass.
  • Recommended next step: Name the owning squad for any suspected XO gap before filing—avoid duplicate tickets across compose stories.
